TextENGNew: Keep going straight ahead, and where the rice paddies end... turn right.
changeReason: Japanese is self-directed instructions ('go straight, turn right'); old translation narrates it as completed past action ('I walked forward... turned right'), losing the sense of Keiichi navigating in real time.

TextENGNew: ...I'd never been here before, and I had no idea where I was going.
I got lost several times, so I may have taken quite a roundabout path.
......Maybe I should have gone home first and come by bicycle...
...Asking my friends would only have made them worry needlessly. ...So I asked a classmate for the location of Satoko's actual house.
That house was originally Satoko's family home, apparently--not her uncle and his wife's.
......But since it was the nicer of the two houses... they had simply taken it over.
Turn right here.
......A row of several houses.@k ......Is it over there...?
...I was visiting Satoko's house, but... I hadn't actually planned on seeing her.
...Nor had I come to reconnoiter the enemy--her supposedly cruel uncle.
......Maybe I just... wanted to be a little closer to her... as her Nii-nii.@k ......Whether that was truly my reason, I wasn't even sure myself.
......If what Tomita-kun and the others told me was correct... was this the house...?
The moment I recognized it...... my feet grew heavy.
...What did... I even come here for?
My real motive for coming here... was pathetic.
......Mion's parting words--'We're powerless'--had left me... frustrated, somehow.
I just wanted to do something, anything, beyond idling away the days praying for a miracle... and that desire had carried my feet all the way here.
...But... that was it.
Even if, hypothetically... I were to encounter the uncle... what could I do?
changeReason: Old adds 'actually tormenting Satoko' not present in this line's JPN, which simply discusses hypothetically encountering the uncle.

TextENGNew: ...Punch the guy out like some manga hero and run off with Satoko?
And then the two of us would live together somewhere far away?
...That was absurd.
......The cicadas sang in chorus... as if mocking me.
...As if telling me to go home already, if I could do nothing and had no intention of trying.
I heard a car approaching, so I stepped aside to let it pass.
...But the car stopped right behind me, and the horn gave one short honk.
Thinking I hadn't moved far enough out of the way, I turned around irritably... only to see a familiar face leaning out of the driver's side window.
``Good afternoon, Maebara-san.@k@vS03/10/141000002. What a coincidence, running into you way out here.@k@vS03/10/141000003. Do you live nearby?``
...My being here at all was far from normal.
...Just as I was trying to think of some excuse, I noticed Satoko sitting in the passenger seat.
Satoko stepped out of the car on unsteady legs. ......From her listless expression and the dullness in her eyes, she clearly wasn't well.
``...I was feeling a bit feverish, so I went to Coach for an examination.@k@vS03/04/SATO04002. ...He offered to drive me home, and I gratefully accepted.``
``......It's probably just a summer cold, but I happened to be heading this way anyway.``
changeReason: Japanese ついでがありました means 'I happened to have other business nearby'; old translation's 'I couldn't just leave it alone' implies a different motivation for the drive.

TextENGNew: As he spoke, Coach was pulling Satoko's bicycle out from where it had been crammed into the back seat.
``...A-A cold...?@k@vS03/01/KEI040107. Then she should be at home resting in bed!``
``......Thanks to Coach... I was saved.@k@vS03/04/140400038. ...The hill was... a bit too much for me.``
Satoko turned a grateful smile toward Coach...@k but it was painfully stiff, and to anyone watching, it was almost heartbreaking...
``Satoko.@k@vS03/01/KEI040109. ......Are you really... okay...?``
``......Yes, I'm quite all right now.@k@vS03/04/SATO04004. ...Besides, I must prepare dinner for my uncle and his guests......``
changeReason: Japanese 叔父さまたち (plural) indicates 'uncle and his companions/guests'; old translation only mentions 'uncle', omitting the others Satoko must serve.

TextENGNew: She's feeling awful... and she's worried about that?
Where was this uncle right now, anyway?
If he couldn't even bother to take her to the clinic, he must be working up a good sweat at this very moment, right...?!
changeReason: Old translation says uncle 'needed to send Satoko out by herself'; Japanese says he couldn't 'accompany' her to the clinic - new translation's 'couldn't even bother to take her' better captures the sarcastic criticism.

TextENGNew: If not... then Satoko had gone to the clinic alone... while feeling this sick...?!
changeReason: Old translation abstracts to generic suffering; Japanese specifically states Satoko went to the clinic alone while sick, which the new translation preserves.

TextENGNew: ...I hadn't meant to say any of that out loud.
......But... it may have shown on my face.
Coach must have sensed it, because he gave me a light pat on the shoulder...
Just then, one of the windows clattered open... and a man with such a thuggish look that I instinctively averted my eyes appeared...
......No introductions were necessary.
...My gut told me instantly... this was the uncle.
His first words to Satoko should have been concern about whether her illness was serious......
changeReason: Old says 'words of gratitude for Satoko'; JPN says his first words should have been concern about her illness severity. Different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Satokoo!!@k@vS03/21/142000061. You left the damn door wide open when you went out, you little--!!``
``...I-I'm sorry...@k@vS03/04/SATO04006. ...My head was foggy, and I just......``
changeReason: Japanese 頭がぼおっとしてて means 'my head was foggy/dazed' (from fever); old translation's 'I was daydreaming' implies inattention rather than illness symptoms.

TextENGNew: ``Shut yer mouth!!``
``...I'm sorry... I'm sorry... I'm sorry...!``
Satoko bowed her head over and over, apologizing profusely...
I... had no idea... what was going on.

``......Oh hey.@k@vS03/21/142000064. If it ain't Doctor Irie!@k@vS03/21/142000065. Well howdy!@k@vS03/21/142000066. What brings ya all the way out here, Doc?@k@vS03/21/142000067. Come on in, come on in! Let's crack open a beer!``
changeReason: Japanese いい酒ば手に入った means 'got some good sake/liquor'; new translation changes this to 'beer' which is a different drink. Also alters the specific offer structure from the original.

TextENGNew: ``......No... I'll have to decline.@k@vS03/10/141000005. I just happened to run into Satoko-chan and gave her a ride home.@k@vS03/10/141000006. Now if you'll excuse me, I have work to attend to.``
``Aww, c'mon~! At least stay for one drink!@k@vS03/21/142000070. Dah-ha-ha-ha-ha!``
......I could barely follow what the uncle and Coach were saying.
But one thing was perfectly clear...... not a single word of what the uncle had said showed the slightest concern for Satoko's health.
``Yer bein' too harsh on the girl, Tecchan.@k@vS03/00/goroA04002. Show her a little kindness, wouldja? Poor thing.``
``Nah, a brat like her needs this much.@k@vS03/21/142000072. ...She's been this way since she was at my brother's place.@k@vS03/21/142000073. Spoil her and she gets ideas.``
``Anyway, it's your draw, Tecchan!``
``Tsubaki-san called riichi.``
``...Oh, my bad.@k@vS03/21/142000019. ......What, you're in riichi again, Tsubaki...?``
It wasn't just the uncle--several of his cronies were there too, it seemed.
...Wait...@k What was he--@k no, what were they all doing...?
Spewing filthy language left and right... sending Satoko to the clinic alone... and doing what, exactly?!
``Uncle's friends came over from town...... and they're having a party.@k@vS03/04/140400049. ...I've been told to prepare dinner for his guests as well.``
``Wha......... what... the hell...?``
A party?!
He invited his friends over... for a party in the middle of the day?!
...They were too busy partying... so they sent an unwell Satoko... to the clinic by herself...
And the first thing he said to her when she got back... was that she left the door open?!
What the hell?! What the actual hell?!
...Coach could hear everything... but he pretended he couldn't.
......He'd apparently let Satoko rest in the back seat during the drive, because he was now folding a blanket and stowing it in the trunk.
``Coach!!@k@vS03/01/140100158. You're going to let this stand?!@k@vS03/01/140100159. This is absolutely--!!``
...Coach pretended he hadn't heard my outburst, either.
......In hindsight, his response was probably the wisest course of action for that moment...
``...Maebara-san, take Satoko-chan's bicycle.@k@vS03/10/IRI04003. ...And these bags--please carry them to the house for her.``
``......Keiichi-san, it's quite all right.@k@vS03/04/SATO04009. I can carry them myself...``
Seeing the anguish I was biting back written on my face, Satoko misread it as annoyance at being saddled with extra work...... and called out to me.
``......Idiot.@k@vS03/01/KEI040111. ......Don't be ridiculous--this is nothing.``
......I forced myself to smile. ......I could only hope it looked convincing.
``...Coach... I'm terribly sorry.@k@vS03/04/SATO04011. ...Going so far out of your way for me...``
``No, not at all...``@k Coach answered with a vague smile.
...Looking perfectly composed.
......But I alone could tell.
...Coach was an adult who knew how to keep his emotions from showing.@k ......But deep in his eyes... there burned a fury equal to or greater than my own...
``...Truly, thank you so very much.@k@vS03/04/140400055. ...Both Coach and Keiichi-san.@k@vS03/04/140400056. I am ever so grateful for your help.``
After carrying everything to the back door, Satoko said that was far enough.
......Every so often, the vulgar laughter of the uncle and his cronies drifted out from inside the house... making my blood boil.
......I knew they weren't laughing at us.
No... and that was precisely why it enraged me.
......It infuriated me from the bottom of my heart that they could ignore Satoko when she was clearly feeling so unwell, and just laugh selfishly to their own amusement.
...Of course, Coach must have felt the same way.
But... if he carelessly let that show on his face... it would only cause Satoko more trouble.
......Knowing all that... as if it didn't bother him at all... he spoke coolly once more.
"......Not at all.@k@vS03/10/141000015. I'm glad I could be of some small help.@k@vS03/10/141000016. ...If there's anything else I can help with, please don't hesitate to say so."
"Yes.@k@vS03/04/140400058. ......Just knowing you feel that way truly makes me happy."
......Just knowing he feels that way.
......Those words... were sad.
......Satoko herself... had resigned herself to the fact that this was as far as we could help... and that... was sad.
...That's right.
......No matter how much Coach and I wanted to help Satoko...@k...this was as far as we could go.@k......The helplessness of not being able to do anything more... was simply... pathetic...
A scorching fury was welling up from deep inside... rising into my throat...
Trembling all over... the fury was about to spill from my eyes...
"......Me too... if there's anything I can do... I'll help whenever you need.@k@vS03/01/140100164. ...So, umm... hang in there..."
At the very least...@kI wanted to at least give her those trite words, saying I'd always be there to help...
"......Yes.@k@vS03/04/140400060. Thank you very much.@k@vS03/04/140400061. ......Just that you came all this way... when you didn't need to... made me happy."
...Satoko seemed to have realized...@kthat my house was on the opposite side of the school...@k...and why I was in a place like this.
"......Well.@k@vS03/04/140400063. You should be going now."
After we'd already gone to the trouble of looking after her... she didn't want us to get tangled up with trouble too.@k...Because she wanted to be the only one to bear this misfortune.
......It looked that way to me... and it was painful.
Unable to bear it any longer, I gently took Satoko's hand.
......And at that moment, I noticed that the heat of Satoko's palm was abnormally high—@k...feverishly so, enough to feel the sweat.
"......Hey, Satoko... you still have a fever...?!"
"......This year's summer cold seems to be quite persistent.@k@vS03/04/SATO04013. Please, pay it no mind."
"......Y-You're lying...!!"
Coach poked me in the back.
Then he pressed his index finger to his lips... chiding me for being too loud.
"Thank you so very much.@k@vS03/04/140400068. ......Well then, tomorrow.@k@vS03/04/140400069. ...I hope we can see each other at school."
"W-We're not talking about that!@k@vS03/01/KEI040114. You should be in bed!@k@vS03/01/KEI040115. ......Ahh... ah...!!"
The very moment my raging fury tried to burst out of my throat... my mouth was clamped firmly shut...
"Mmmnff!! Nnmmnggmff!!!"
Coach... from behind... had wrapped both hands firmly over my mouth, holding it shut.
So I couldn't form words.
...I could only groan.@k...But I groaned.@kGroaning was all I could do.
If I didn't expel all the magma from the pit of my stomach here and now...@k...I would surely explode into a million pieces on the spot...
I groaned.@kI groaned.
From my mouth.
From my whole body.
While hot tears spilled from my eyes.
......As though she were a completely different person from the girl we'd just been talking to.
......I screamed.
......I wrung every last drop from the depths of my gut.
......Even after my voice gave out... I kept on wringing... and wringing...

``......I understand how you feel...!!@k@vS03/10/141000018. I really do, Maebara-san!!@k@vS03/10/141000019. ...Just for now... just for this moment, please bear it...!@k@vS03/10/141000020. ...Please...!!``
Bear it for now?!@k Bear it, and then what?!@k What about Satoko... right in front of us?!@k After what they've put her through...@k ...And I can't even... raise my voice against it?!
Coach!!@k Are we really this powerless?! So powerless we can't even... scream from the bottom of our hearts?!@k Is that how helpless we are?!
......The call of the higurashi cicadas...... cooled my frenzy.
I didn't know... how much time had passed.
Satoko... slowly... gave a small, gentle bow.
``......Thank you so much, Keiichi-san.``

``......Just now... you looked just like my real Nii-nii, Keiichi-san.@k@vS03/04/140400074. ...My real Nii-nii... is gone now, but.@k@vS03/04/140400075. ......I think... my real Nii-nii... would have screamed just like that for me too.``

``......That's right...@k@vS03/01/140100176. ...I'm... your Nii-nii.@k@vS03/01/140100177. ...When things get hard for you... I'll absolutely come to help you...@k@vS03/01/140100178. ......I promise...``
...Satoshi Houjou... you goddamn fool!!
It's now, isn't it?!@k Right now is when Satoko... needs you the most!!

If her Nii-nii won't save her now... then when will he?!@k Why did you run?!@k Why did you abandon her?!@k Why did you leave Satoko behind?!@k You don't... deserve to call yourself... Satoko's Nii-nii!!!
``......I... won't run away...``

``...I will never...... run away.@k@vS03/01/140100181. ...I won't abandon you and flee... like Satoshi did!@k@vS03/01/140100182. I swear it...!!@k@vS03/01/140100183. I absolutely... will never... run away...!!!``
A single, quiet streak.
......From Satoko's eyes...... a tear traced down her cheek.
And then... she narrowed her eyes... and smiled.
Satoko lifted her right hand... and gave an awkward little wave.

``......I'll be all right.@k@vS03/04/140400080. ...What my Nii-nii just did... gave me... so very, very much strength.@k@vS03/04/140400081. ...I'll be fine...@k@vS03/04/140400082. ...I can keep going...@k@vS03/04/140400083. ......Thank you.``
``I'll be okay...@k@vS03/04/140400084. ......So, you see.@k@vS03/04/140400085. ......For today... this is... where we say good-bye.``
There was no way... I could say something so carefree as 'See you tomorrow.'
At that moment... from deep inside the house... came a coarse, repulsive voice.
``......Let's go, Maebara-san.@k@vS03/10/141000022. ...Staying any longer... would only cause trouble for Satoko-chan.``
``...Coach.@k@vS03/04/140400091. ...Nii-nii's house... is rather far away.@k@vS03/04/140400092. ...If it wouldn't be too much trouble... could you give him a ride...?``
``......Of course.@k@vS03/10/141000024. I'll take him home.``
...Coach grabbed me by the back of my collar... and with a strength I never could have imagined from him... began dragging me away.
......And I... unable to resist... grew farther and farther from Satoko.
Satoko waved to us with that same clouded smile...@k and then disappeared... through the back door.
Coach got into the car first, turned around on the narrow road, and brought the passenger door around to my side.
``......I understand how you feel so acutely it hurts.@k@vS03/10/141000026. ...No... I believe I understand it even more than you do...``

``......But please, just for now, bear it.@k@vS03/10/141000028. ...Your feelings reached her. I'm certain of it.@k@vS03/10/141000029. ......So... please endure.@k@vS03/10/141000030. ...For Satoko-chan's sake.``
``...I... I know already.@k@vS03/01/140100186. .........I'm sorry. And... thank you for earlier.``
.........I meekly got into the passenger seat.
...The car glided forward in silence... and Satoko's house grew smaller and smaller in the rearview mirror.
......After a wide turn... it was gone from view...
``.........Hm?@k@vS03/10/141000032. Is your house really that far?@k@vS03/10/141000033. It's on the other side of town, isn't it?``
......I didn't reply.
...I wouldn't have been able to explain what I was doing near Satoko's house in the first place.
Coach didn't press the matter any further.
For a while... the two of us sat in silence, listening to nothing but gravel crunching beneath the tires.
......I remembered Rena saying that things were different from last year.
Back then, Satoko had Satoshi.
......Satoshi had shielded Satoko.
...So the sparks that fell on Satoko had at least been fewer.
``............But now... Satoshi isn't here.``
``......Yes.@k@vS03/10/141000035. ......It's cruel... but that is the reality.``
......Coach seemed to understand the full weight of what I meant.
``......It's a sad... terribly cruel truth...@k@vS03/10/141000037. but our resolve, our efforts, our dedication... all have limits.``
...It sounded like an excuse... but he was right.
......No matter how much Coach and I wanted to be there for Satoko...@k This was as far as we could go.@k The front door was our limit.
No matter how loudly I proclaimed... that I would be her Nii-nii.
...No matter how fervently I swore never to abandon her, and no matter how willingly Satoko called me Nii-nii.@k ......Beyond this point... there was nothing more I could do.
``...Where on earth...... has Satoshi gone?``
...Right--Satoshi was a different matter entirely.
......Her real brother, Satoshi... was in a position to protect her far, far better than any of us ever could.
......That was the harsh, painful truth... the insurmountable gulf between Satoshi and the rest of us.
``............I wonder...@k@vS03/10/141000039. where he is and what he's doing right now.``
Satoshi had left home.
......Whether it was Oyashiro-sama's curse or just a disappearance, I didn't know the exact reason.
...But the fact remained that he wasn't at Satoko's side now.
...At the time when Satoko needed protection most, he wasn't here.
``......Why isn't he here?!@k@vS03/01/140100190. At the time when she needs him most!``
``......People say all sorts of things about Oyashiro-sama's curse...@k@vS03/10/141000041. but the only thing I can say is...@k@vS03/10/141000042. he ran away from home.``
``A friend of mine said that one day he just never came back.@k@vS03/01/140100192. ...He ran away from home... without leaving his sister Satoko so much as a note...?!``
``...Satoshi-kun had been working part-time jobs diligently.@k@vS03/10/141000044. ...At the time, Satoko-chan desperately wanted a very large stuffed animal.``
``...It was quite expensive, apparently, so he was doing all sorts of odd jobs to save up for it.@k@vS03/10/141000046. ...He was a brother who truly loved his sister.``
``Everyone whispered about how he wanted to give his sister, suffering under their cruel aunt and uncle, at least a wonderful birthday present.``
......In the end... those savings were never used for Satoko's birthday present.
``...After Satoshi-kun disappeared, the police investigated.@k@vS03/10/141000049. They found that he had withdrawn all his savings on the day he vanished.``
``...After checking various leads... they concluded that he had likely used the money to go to Tokyo.``
``......All I can say...@k@vS03/10/141000052. is that he ran away from home...``

``He'd been saving up money just so he could run away.@k@vS03/01/140100196. ...That's selfish enough on its own!@k@vS03/01/140100197. But... he didn't have to leave Satoko behind!@k@vS03/01/140100198. ...If he was going to run... he should have taken her with him!!``
......Even now... Coach remained calm.
...He was careful never to pour fuel on the fire of my anger.
......He simply waited... for it to cool on its own.
After a while of listening to gravel crunch beneath the wheels... Coach spoke quietly.
``......Please try to understand.@k@vS03/10/141000054. ...Satoko-chan, left behind, reached the same conclusion...``
She was a burden on her brother... so he abandoned her.
......He hadn't taken her with him.
......How devastating must it have been for Satoko... to feel betrayed by the brother she loved so dearly...
``But... Satoko-chan was incredibly strong.@k@vS03/10/141000056. ...Of course, she had the support of close friends, but... she was able to smile again.``
I recalled how Satoko had spoken of Satoshi without a trace of shadow... when we'd all had dinner together at my house...
At the time, I'd thought nothing more of it than... what a heartwarming bond between siblings.
......Now, looking back... I realized... it was something like resignation... built upon layer after layer of heartbreaking endurance.
``...She said Satoshi-kun would definitely come back.@k@vS03/10/141000058. ...And that when he did, she wanted to show him that she was no longer a burden.``
``Satoko isn't a burden!!@k@vS03/01/140100200. She's... she's far more capable at daily life than I am... she can handle almost anything!``
``Sure... maybe she falls short of Rena or Rika-chan in some ways... but she's way more self-sufficient than most people!``
``...That's the result of her working tirelessly to be ready for whenever Satoshi might return.@k@vS03/10/141000060. ...She put in heartrending effort.``
While living with Rika-chan...
...While learning from Rika-chan, who could handle everything so capably...
...So that she'd be ready whenever Satoshi came home.
......So she could show him someone he wouldn't be ashamed of.
``......So... when exactly is Satoshi coming back?``
There was no way Coach could answer that.
......The fact remained: Satoshi had fled.
...He had left Satoko behind and fled from Hinamizawa.
......Someone once said that those who abandon Hinamizawa and flee are struck by Oyashiro-sama's curse.
......Then... that would make sense.
...Satoshi's departure, his disappearance... it wouldn't be strange for people to call it the curse...
......And they could smile about it... as if it served him right... as if he got what he deserved.
......Hey, Oyashiro-sama, or whatever you are.
...Satoshi didn't vanish because he ran away on his own... you spirited him away with your curse, didn't you?
......If that's not what happened... then I'll never forgive you.
``......When exactly did Satoshi run away?``

``...The night of the Watanagashi Festival last year,@k@vS03/10/141000063. after their aunt was killed by that deranged man,@k@vS03/10/141000064. ...a few days later.@k@vS03/10/141000065. ......On Satoko-chan's birthday.``
``...On Satoko's... birthday...?!``
BANG!!@k I slammed both palms against the dashboard with all my might.
......Satoshi, you bastard...... of all the days... you chose to run away on...!!
The aunt had just died a few days before, hadn't she?
The days of torment were finally supposed to be over, weren't they?
...It was supposed to be the dawn of a new life, wasn't it?
It's wrong to celebrate someone's death... but for Satoko and her brother... that birthday should have been a turning point.
Their cruel uncle had fled to the city, and the siblings would finally be able to live on their own.
......They should have been able to celebrate that together...@k on Satoko's birthday.
...Satoko must have been waiting... waiting for her brother to come home.
......Waiting and waiting for a brother who never returned.
...Waiting with swelling hopes and dreams of the new life they'd build together... once he finally came back...
......But no matter how long she waited, he didn't return.@k Not even a message.
Satoko, her heart full of anticipation for a wonderful present...... when did she have to face... that merciless reality?
......Satoshi...... you goddamn fool......
I could feel the blood rushing to my head... fueled by my rage at Satoshi...
I was humiliated.
I was furious.
......Satoko still respected and adored her brother...... and his heartless betrayal...... was unforgivable.
......Someone like that...... wouldn't just conveniently come back now.
......That's right--no one would protect her.
...Not even her real brother, Satoshi.
``......That's why... when she came to see you as her Nii-nii... I was so happy.``

``...I too am someone who prays for Satoko-chan's happiness... but I have a position in society and a job that occupies my days.@k@vS03/10/141000068. ...The time I can devote to Satoko-chan is limited.``
``...But you're different.@k@vS03/10/141000070. ...You share school life with her, and you're nearly the same age as Satoko-chan and Satoshi-kun.``
``...I know you think there's a limit to what you can do...@k@vS03/10/141000072. but even so, you're in a position to reach out to Satoko-chan far more than I ever could.``
``...So... please be there for Satoko-chan, enough for the both of us.@k@vS03/10/141000074. That is my heartfelt wish.``
``...But that's...@k@vS03/01/140100208. ......Coach, you could also...``

``Of course, there are things that only someone with my experience and resources can do.@k@vS03/10/141000076. Likewise, there are things that only you can do.@k@vS03/10/141000077. ...That's all I'm saying.``
......Was that a bit too abstract...?
Coach added that caveat... but I shook my head.
Hearing him mention his 'experience' reminded me that he was an adult--someone in a fundamentally different position from us.
......Maybe it would be worth asking him.
......I thought back to the argument I'd had with Mion and the others about Satoko.
Our debate as children had ended at 'wait and see,' but what would an adult's perspective yield...?
``......Satoko-chan... might not want that.``
``Satoko... wouldn't want it?!@k@vS03/01/140100213. Why...?``
Coach didn't answer.
He wasn't pretending not to hear.
......It was more like... he wanted to explain but couldn't find the right words.
``...Satoko-chan probably... views enduring this kind of treatment as a trial.``
``A trial?@k@vS03/01/140100215. ...This isn't some religious ordeal...!!``

``...As you know, Satoshi-kun always shielded Satoko-chan.@k@vS03/10/141000086. ...And Satoko-chan always depended on Satoshi-kun.@k@vS03/10/141000087. ...She believes that dependence is what drove him away.``
I already knew that Satoshi had always protected Satoko from their aunt and uncle.
But that was simply his duty as her older brother--hardly worth making a fuss over...
``...That may have become a trauma for her.@k@vS03/10/141000089. ...She may have concluded that she leaned on her brother too heavily, and that's why he grew to resent her.``
For Satoko, the current situation was a near-exact replay of just over a year ago.
...Only this time, Satoshi wasn't here...

``...So she wants to overcome this trial by herself, without relying on anyone.@k@vS03/10/141000091. She believes that unless she develops that strength, Satoshi-kun will never come back.@k@vS03/10/141000092. ...She's determined to prove she can stand on her own.``
.........Now that he mentioned it...
...Not once had Satoko complained about how hard things were.
......Given Satoko's prideful personality, always refusing to admit defeat... that was entirely in character.

``Living in that house and waiting for Satoshi-kun to return is Satoko-chan's reason for living.@k@vS03/10/IRI04007. ......A girl abandoned by her brother... try to imagine what she found to live for.``
``...Try to see it through Satoko-chan's eyes, not your own.``
......I felt like I was being lost in one of those roundabout, hard-to-follow explanations that only adults could give.
In short... since Satoko herself wanted to stay in that house, there was nothing to be done...
So... I was supposed to just watch... until she broke down.
...In the end...@k no matter how he dressed it up... Coach's answer was also... wait and see.
``I...... don't understand.``
``...Pardon?``
``...If... if I ever feel that Satoko is truly in danger... I will act, without consulting anyone.``
``......She might resent me for it... and the result might make her unhappy.........@k@vS03/01/KEI040118. ...But I believe... that in the end, it will lead to Satoko's happiness.``
Coach turned to look at me...... and stared intently into my eyes.
......I could read it on his face--his astonishment... at what a child my age had just said.
``.........Why... would you go that far...?``
...Coach... no, perhaps any ordinary adult... might not understand.
So I told him, plainly and simply, the creed and values I believed in.
``Satoko is my friend.@k@vS03/01/140100232. ...And I am Satoko's Nii-nii... Is that not reason enough...?``
I held his slightly wavering gaze with eyes of iron--unbreakable, unbending.
``..................... ...No... of course that's more than enough.``
...After a long silence, Coach finally managed those words.
``We made a promise, didn't we?``
``...Um............ I'm sorry... what was it...?``
``That we would never let Satoko cry.``
That day at the barbecue party, we had sworn it together.
......We hadn't thought too deeply about it at the time... but Coach and I had made a promise all the same.
...That we would never, ever let Satoko cry.
Coach gave a rueful smile and immediately apologized for not remembering.
......That rueful smile... made me wonder, just for a moment... whether he lacked just a fraction of the passion needed to truly save Satoko...
The brakes gave a light squeal, and my whole body lurched forward.
``......Could that be your house over there?@k@vS03/10/141000118. Oh... so the Maebara estate is your home. Of course.``
......Before I knew it... we were almost at my house.
